era: Seattle, 1990s underground. Active 1993-2001. Drone/noise duo. Heavy, hypnotic, instrumental. No vocals, no conventional song structure.

location.formative: 1990s Seattle underground. Operating alongside grunge but at the extreme edge. Drone/noise instrumental duo.
location.career: Hovercraft -- drone/noise duo of Beth Liebling (bass) and Ryan Campbell (guitar, electronics). Beth Liebling married to Eddie Vedder (Pearl Jam, degree 83). Tag and DGC Records (Geffen subsidiary). Drone/Noise at Genre Stage 73. E=2. First degree of Quadrant II.

members.name: Beth Liebling
members.note: Co-founder. Married to Eddie Vedder (Pearl Jam, degree 83). Bass as drone instrument.

members.name: Ryan Campbell
members.note: Co-founder. Guitar feedback, electronic processing, distortion.

associated_musicians.name: Pearl Jam
associated_musicians.note: Beth Liebling married to Eddie Vedder. Direct personal connection. Same household, opposite aesthetics.

associated_musicians.name: Sky Cries Mary
associated_musicians.note: Both 1990s Seattle underground outside grunge. Sky Cries Mary ethereal/electronic; Hovercraft drone/noise.

associated_musicians.name: Jimi Hendrix
associated_musicians.note: Hendrix pioneered feedback and distortion. Hovercraft takes that lineage to its logical extreme.

associated_musicians.name: Phil Elverum
associated_musicians.note: Both use drone and texture. Elverum within song structures; Hovercraft abandons structure entirely.

associated_musicians.name: Parenthetical Girls
associated_musicians.note: Both experimental. Parenthetical Girls theatrical avant-pop; Hovercraft instrumental drone/noise.

theory_nodes.concept: QUADRANT II OPENS -- degree 91 is the first degree of Quadrant II (91-180); the engine passes the quarter-mark; opens with the most extreme genre yet
examples: Degree 91 = first degree of Quadrant II. The engine passes the quarter-mark.
The second quadrant (91-180) opens with Drone/Noise -- the most extreme genre yet.
Quadrant I mapped the foundation. Quadrant II builds on it.
Opens at the edge: E=2, no vocals, no structure, maximum sonic extremity.
theory_nodes.description: QUADRANT II OPENS at degree 91. First degree of the second quadrant. Opens with Drone/Noise -- the most extreme genre yet.

ancestors: Quarter-mark at degree 90. First quadrant complete.
genealogy.relationship: Quadrant II begins. Track through degree 180 (half-mark).

theory_nodes.concept: The sustained crush (ninety-second acoustic element -- Hovercraft, sustained bass drones, guitar feedback loops, electronic distortion, physical pressure, the sound presses down like weight, constant pressure)
examples: Hovercraft (degree 91) -- sustained bass drones, guitar feedback, electronic distortion. The sustained crush.
Contrast with the ambient drift (degree 90, Sky Cries Mary) -- floats gently. The sustained crush presses and overwhelms.
The engine moves from floating (90) to crushing (91). From the planetarium to the bunker.
Both immersive but through opposite mechanisms: drift through gentleness, crush through force.
theory_nodes.description: The sustained crush is the ninety-second acoustic element. Sustained drones, feedback, distortion. Physical pressure. The sound presses down.

ancestors: The ambient drift (degree 90, Sky Cries Mary) -- gentle immersion. The sustained crush is forceful immersion.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-two acoustic elements across ninety-one degrees. Track element 93 at degree 92.

theory_nodes.concept: The spousal satellite (ninety-second artist-city pattern -- a project in the biographical orbit of a famous partner; Beth Liebling/Eddie Vedder; artistic independence and biographical proximity coexist; proximity to fame does not guarantee cultural reach)
examples: Hovercraft (degree 91) -- Beth Liebling's drone/noise project, married to Eddie Vedder (Pearl Jam, degree 83). The spousal satellite.
The spousal satellite has its own artistic identity but is discussed in relation to the famous partner.
Artistic independence and biographical proximity coexist.
Proximity to fame does not guarantee cultural reach. E=2.
theory_nodes.description: The spousal satellite is the ninety-second artist-city pattern. A project in the orbit of a famous partner. Independence despite proximity.

ancestors: The parallel scene (degree 90, Sky Cries Mary) -- operating alongside dominance. The spousal satellite is connected to dominance by marriage.
Direct connection to Pearl Jam (degree 83) through Vedder/Liebling.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-two artist-city patterns across ninety-one degrees. Track pattern 93 at degree 92.

theory_nodes.concept: Drone/Noise at Genre Stage 73 -- the most extreme genre yet; no vocals, no structure, no melody; sustained tones, feedback, distortion; hypnotic through density
examples: Hovercraft (degree 91) -- Drone/Noise at Genre Stage 73.
The most extreme genre the engine has catalogued. No vocals. No song structure.
Distinguished from ethereal/electronic (90) and experimental electronic (44).
Drone/Noise: sustained tones, feedback, distortion as the entire music.
theory_nodes.description: Drone/Noise at Genre Stage 73. The most extreme genre yet. No vocals, no structure. Sustained tones and feedback.

ancestors: Ethereal/Electronic (degree 90) at Genre Stage 72. Drone/Noise is more extreme.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 73 at degree 91. Track at 92.

theory_nodes.concept: E=2 at degree 91 -- quiet in cultural reach, LOUD in volume; maximum sonic intensity, minimum cultural penetration; the drone/noise paradox
examples: Hovercraft (degree 91) -- E=2. The volume paradox.
Quiet in cultural reach, LOUD in volume.
Maximum sonic intensity, minimum cultural penetration.
The drone/noise paradox: the loudest music with the smallest audience.
theory_nodes.description: E=2 at degree 91. The volume paradox. Quiet culturally, LOUD sonically. Maximum intensity, minimum penetration.

ancestors: E=3 at degree 90 (Sky Cries Mary). Drops to E=2 at 91.
The energy drops as extremity increases.

theory_nodes.concept: Tag Records and DGC Records (Geffen subsidiary) -- a noise band on a major-label subsidiary; the 1990s economics of signing extreme acts; post-Nirvana signing frenzy
examples: Hovercraft (degree 91) -- Tag Records and DGC Records.
DGC = Geffen subsidiary. A drone/noise band on a major-label subsidiary.
The post-Nirvana signing frenzy: majors signed extreme acts hoping for the next breakthrough.
Tag Records: independent. DGC: corporate. Both served Hovercraft.
theory_nodes.description: Tag Records and DGC Records (Geffen subsidiary). Noise on a major subsidiary. Post-Nirvana signing economics.

ancestors: Seattle's label ecosystem: Sub Pop, Barsuk, K Records, Rockville, now Tag/DGC.
genealogy.relationship: Track Seattle label diversity. Major-label subsidiaries enter the map.

cross_references.relationship: Vedder/Liebling marriage. Beth Liebling (Hovercraft) married to Eddie Vedder (Pearl Jam). Direct personal connection. Same household, opposite aesthetics. The spousal satellite pattern.
1990s Seattle underground. Both outside grunge. Sky Cries Mary ethereal/electronic (ambient drift); Hovercraft drone/noise (sustained crush). Two flavors of the underground.
Noise lineage. Hendrix pioneered feedback and distortion as musical tools. Hovercraft takes that lineage to its extreme -- feedback and distortion as the entire music.
Drone/texture. Both use drone and texture. Elverum within song structures; Hovercraft abandons structure. Drone as element vs drone as totality.
Experimental. Both experimental. Parenthetical Girls theatrical avant-pop; Hovercraft instrumental drone/noise. Opposite directions of experimentation.
